summary: Round-1 fixes substantially resolve the prior findings. M1 (rotation litmus) is addressed via a documented Robolectric limitation — Robolectric's createBitmap does not rasterise matrix-transformed pixels, so pixel-motion assertions are infeasible in this harness. The switched-to assertNotSame(src, out) still catches the most common regression class (matrix mutation deleted entirely → identity matrix → createBitmap short-circuit returns source), and the limitation plus backstop (device smoke AC-13/AC-18) is documented in the test class KDoc. M2 is fully resolved — FLIP_VERTICAL, TRANSPOSE, TRANSVERSE branches now have explicit tests, and the two new dim-swapping orientations carry a strong litmus (identity matrix cannot swap dimensions). m1 is fully resolved — MediaUtilsPureTest.kt is a separate, Robolectric-free file (0.003s vs MediaUtilsTest's 2.968s demonstrates the D20 seam is now realised at the code level, not just documented). m2 partially resolved (description truncation covered; log rotation deferred with defensible YAGNI given fixture cost and absence from the TDD anchor list). m3 is fully resolved (assertSame replaces assertEquals in AudioClip reference test). All 55 core-runtime unit tests pass at HEAD. The residual litmus gap for dim-preserving rotations (180 / flipH / flipV are mutually indistinguishable in assertions) is a real but narrow limitation of the Robolectric harness; the one minor recommendation below addresses it non-blockingly.

findings.location: core-runtime/src/androidTest/ (does not exist) — complementary to core-runtime/src/test/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/common/MediaUtilsTest.kt:107-142 (rotate180 / flipH / flipV)
findings.issue: The three dim-preserving orientations (ROTATE_180, FLIP_HORIZONTAL, FLIP_VERTICAL) are tested only by dim equality + assertNotSame(src, out). This correctly catches 'matrix mutation deleted' regressions (createBitmap full-region + identity short-circuit returns source), but cannot distinguish between the three ops: if the FLIP_HORIZONTAL branch were silently copy-pasted to also run for FLIP_VERTICAL, both tests would still pass. Dev's KDoc correctly identifies the Robolectric limitation and points to AC-13 / AC-18 device smoke as the pixel-level backstop. The workaround is acceptable for Phase 2, but the backstop is manual, not automated. There is no androidTest source set in :core-runtime today.

Log rotation (file > 2 MB → rename to errors.log.1, delete existing .1) still has no test. Dev's justification (2 MB fixture cost, not on TDD anchor list, manual smoke covers) is reasonable for Phase 2, but the branch is not trivial: it mixes a size check, conditional delete of a pre-existing rotated file, and a rename. A silent regression (e.g., rename order reversed, existing .1 not deleted → rename fails silently) would go unnoticed until a user hits 2 MB in production. Round-1 finding m2 explicitly called this out as a gap; dev's response is 'deferred', not 'resolved'.
